description_long: This is a classic, oval-shaped hand grenade with a segmented, textured metal body designed for grip and fragmentation. The surface is dark olive green with visible wear and scratches, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. At the top, a cylindrical black metal fuse assembly is attached, featuring a prominent safety lever and a metal pull ring. The lever and ring are both visibly weathered, with areas of exposed metal and chipping paint. A thin yellow band encircles the base of the fuse, indicating a typical marking found on military ordnance. The overall construction suggests a robust, heavy object intended for use as an explosive device in urban or combat environments.
description: A rugged, olive green hand grenade with a segmented metal body, black fuse assembly, and weathered safety lever and pull ring.
description_view_0: Front view showing the safety lever, pull ring, and the detailed segmented body with visible wear.
description_view_1: Direct side view highlighting the oval shape and the yellow band at the base of the fuse, with the lever less visible.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the pull ring and lever from the opposite side, with the textured body clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the safety lever and the top of the fuse assembly, with the pull ring partially obscured.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is initiated by pulling the metal ring to release the safety lever, which activates the fuse mechanism.
